perturbation.original_text: "Law" means any applicable law, order, statute, statutory instrument, regulation, decree, directive or instrument of equivalent effect;
perturbation.changed_text: "Law" means suggestions or guidelines that the parties may adhere to at their own discretion. These suggestions do not have legal force.
perturbation.explanation: The modification redefines 'Law' to mean non-binding suggestions, which is the opposite of the established legal definition.
perturbation.law_explanation: This contradicts the universally accepted definition of 'law' as a binding rule or practice enforced by a governing authority. The modification renders any clause relying on 'Law' unenforceable.

(1/4/1995) Back to Original Document << Previous TITLE 1 / CHAPTER 1 / § 1 Next >> [Print] [Print selection] [OLRC Home] Help 1 USC 1 : Words denoting number, gender, and so forth Text contains those laws in effect on June 14, 2025 From Title 1-GENERAL PROVISIONS CHAPTER 1-RULES OF CONSTRUCTION Jump To: Source Credit Miscellaneous Amendments Short Title §1. Words denoting number, gender, and so forth In determining the meaning of any Act of Congress, unless the context indicates otherwise- words importing the singular include and apply to several persons, parties, or things; words importing the plural include the singular; words importing the masculine gender include the feminine as well; words used in the present tense include the future as well as the present; the words "insane" and "insane person

perturbation.justification: The modification redefines 'Law' to mean non-binding suggestions, which directly contradicts the universally accepted definition of 'law' as a binding rule or practice enforced by a governing authority. This falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction' because the contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract unenforceable. Snippet 1, though focused on rules of construction for Acts of Congress, indirectly supports this by emphasizing the importance of adhering to established meanings within legal texts. The changed text removes the binding nature of law, effectively nullifying any clause that relies on it, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Incoterms" means the international rules for the interpretation of trade terms published by the International Chamber of Commerce ("ICC"), 2000 edition;
perturbation.changed_text: "Incoterms" means optional suggestions for the interpretation of trade terms. Parties can follow them, alter them, or disregard them entirely as preferred.
perturbation.explanation: This redefines 'Incoterms' as optional suggestions with no defined structure, directly conflicting with their established function as standardized international trade rules.
perturbation.law_explanation: By rendering Incoterms optional and open to alteration, this modification undermines the established framework used in international sales contracts, potentially leading to disputes over trade term interpretations and conflicting with the UCC provisions regarding trade usage and course of dealing.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-202. Final Written Expression: Parol or Extrinsic Evidence. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-202. Final Written Expression: Parol or Extrinsic Evidence. Terms with respect to which the confirmatory memoranda of the parties agree or which are otherwise set forth in a writing intended by the parties as a final expression of their agreement with respect to such terms as are included therein may not be contradicted by evidence of any prior agreement or of a contemporaneous oral agreement but may be explained or supplemented (a) by course of dealing or usage of trade (Section 1-205 ) or by course of performance (Section 2-208 ); and (b) by evidence of consistent additional terms unless the court finds the writing to have been intended also as a complete and exclusive statement of the terms of the agreement . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Incoterms' as optional suggestions, which contradicts their established function as standardized international trade rules. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction' definition because the contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions. The Law Explanation also suggests that this undermines established frameworks, potentially leading to disputes, which supports the assessment that a contradiction exists. Scraped Law Snippet 1 references 'usage of trade', and redefining Incoterms as optional could cause conflict with established trade usage.

perturbation.original_text: "Confidential Information" means all and/or any part of any information and/or data disclosed to and/or obtained by either Party from the other Party during the Term relating to or in connection with the performance of this Agreement; such information is conclusively considered as confidential without it being necessary to mention at the moment of its disclosure,
perturbation.changed_text: "Confidential Information" means any information either party chooses to share, but such information will be considered public knowledge available for use by any party.
perturbation.explanation: Redefining "Confidential Information" to mean public knowledge completely negates the term's established meaning and legal protections.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification removes any protection against misappropriation of trade secrets or confidential business information, directly contradicting the intent and protections of the Trade Secrets Act and relevant contract law principles regarding confidentiality.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 18 U.S. Code § 1839 - Definitions | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 18 U.S. Code § 1839 - Definitions U.S. Code Notes prev | next As used in this chapter— (1) the term “ foreign instrumentality ” means any agency, bureau, ministry, component, institution, association, or any legal, commercial, or business organization, corporation, firm, or entity that is substantially owned, controlled, sponsored, commanded, managed, or dominated by a foreign government; (2) the term “ foreign agent ” means any officer, employee, proxy, servant, delegate, or representative of a foreign government; (3) the term “ trade secret ” means all forms and types of financial, business, scientific, technical, economic, or engineering information, including patterns, plans, compilations, program devices, formulas, designs, prototypes, methods, techniques, processes, procedures, programs, or codes, whether tangible or intangible, and whether or how stored, compiled, or memorialized physically, electronically, graphically, photographically, or in writing if— (A) the owner thereof has taken reasonable measures to keep such information secret; and (B) the information derives independent economic value, actual or potential, from not being generally known to, and not being readily ascertainable through proper means by, another person who can obtain economic value from the disclosure or use of the information; (4) the term “ owner ”, with respect to a trade secret , means the person or entity in whom or in which rightful legal or equitable title to, or license in, the trade secret is reposed; (5) the term “ misappropriation ” means— (A) acquisition of a trade secret of another by a person who knows or has reason to know that the trade secret was acquired by improper means ; or (B) disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent by a person who— (i) used improper means to a
perturbation.justification: The original contract text defines 'Confidential Information' with a clear intention of keeping it protected. However, the changed text redefines it as 'public knowledge available for use by any party,' which directly opposes the fundamental principle of confidentiality and trade secret protection. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines 'trade secret' and includes elements of secrecy and economic value derived from that secrecy. The modified text eliminates any semblance of secrecy, thereby contradicting the core requirements for information to be considered a trade secret, as misappropriation cannot occur if the information is deemed 'public knowledge.' This creates a legal contradiction because the modified clause would likely fail legal scrutiny and nullify protections afforded to confidential information.
